

![][image1]  
![][image2]![][image3]![][image4]

Notice: 

1) The wrist/top camera currently being used is only a reference setup. It is a global-shutter industrial camera designed for capturing fast-moving objects. For the SO101, a regular rolling-shutter camera is generally also suitable.  
     
   However, for the wrist camera, it is important to consider that it captures objects at a close distance. Therefore, the camera should have an appropriate focal length so that nearby objects can still be clearly observed.  
     
2) In the current setup, data cables of at least 2 meters are needed for both the wrist/top cameras and the follower arm.  
     
   

- [ ] Front Camera  
      [WebCamera](https://www.amazon.se/Webbkamera-Onlineklassvideo-Livekamera-Datorkamera-Konferenser/dp/B0B15KFLKT/ref=asc_df_B0B15KFLKT?mcid=ca48fdb318fe3047a64a3ae2516f1718&tag=shpngadsglede-21&linkCode=df0&hvadid=768970337198&hvpos=&hvnetw=g&hvrand=14543025911038805189&hvpone=&hvptwo=&hvqmt=&hvdev=c&hvdvcmdl=&hvlocint=&hvlocphy=9197457&hvtargid=pla-2384979561550&psc=1&language=sv_SE&gad_source=1)  
      Already have 2\.  
        
      ![][image5]  
- [ ] Top Camera  
      Notice: There are two cameras of the same model as the front camera, and one can be used as the top camera.  
      ![][image6]![][image7]  
      ![][image8]![][image9]  
        
        
- [ ] Wrist Camera  
      Notice: For the wrist camera, it is better to use a **softer and more flexible cable**. The connector on the camera side should preferably use **pin headers** to avoid the connection becoming loose after long-term use or repeated movement.  
      ![][image10]![][image11]  
        
       ![][image12]  
        
- [ ] Wrist Camera Mount [https://github.com/TheRobotStudio/SO-ARM100/tree/main/Optional](https://github.com/TheRobotStudio/SO-ARM100/tree/main/Optional) You can find the module that best fits your camera, download the STL file, and slice the model using Bambu Studio (if you are using a Bambu Lab printer). If your camera has a different shape or size, import the original STL file into Blender, modify the model as needed, and check online Blender tutorials. It’s simple and quick."
